validation.comparison_result: 3 row(s) differ - zone map pruning logic is inverted (line 187: skips blocks with max_date < cutoff when filter is o_orderdate < cutoff, should skip blocks with min_date >= cutoff)

Q3.notes: Validation failed: 3/10 rows differ. Root cause: zone map pruning logic at line 187 is inverted - it skips blocks where max_date < date_cutoff, but the filter is 'o_orderdate < date_cutoff', so it's skipping the exact blocks we need! Should skip blocks where min_date >= date_cutoff instead.

Q3.bottleneck: Zone map pruning is inverted, causing wrong results. After fixing correctness: orders_filter_join is the dominant operation, consuming 50.7% of execution time.
Q3.root_cause: Line 187: zone_map[m].second < date_cutoff skips blocks where max_date is before cutoff, but we WANT those blocks (filter is o_orderdate < cutoff). This causes ~50% of qualifying orders to be missed, producing wrong results and incorrect timing measurements.

analysis.overall: Query produces wrong results due to inverted zone map pruning logic in orders scan. The zone map skip condition at line 187 is backwards: it skips blocks where all dates are BEFORE the cutoff (max < cutoff), when those are exactly the blocks that pass the filter (o_orderdate < cutoff). Correct logic should skip blocks where min_date >= cutoff. Once fixed, performance target is DuckDB's 77.8-112.8ms range (currently 197.7ms, ~2.5x slower). After correctness fix, main bottlenecks will be: (1) orders_filter_join 50.7%, (2) lineitem_scan_aggregation 32.3%, (3) customer_filter 11.3%.

critical_fixes.issue: Zone map pruning logic is inverted - skipping wrong blocks

performance_optimizations.description: After fixing correctness: orders_filter_join (100.3ms, 50.7%) builds a hash table from filtered orders (o_custkey in customer_ht AND o_orderdate < cutoff). Current approach: 64 threads scan orders in parallel, build thread-local hash tables (lines 182-203), then merge into single orders_ht (lines 206-212). Optimization: Eliminate the merge step by using a concurrent hash table (e.g., folly::ConcurrentHashMap or tbb::concurrent_unordered_map) where all threads insert directly. This removes the 10-15ms merge overhead and reduces memory footprint. Expected improvement: ~10-15ms (reduce orders_filter_join from 100ms to 85-90ms).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: ~10-15ms (10-15% reduction in orders_filter_join time) - eliminates hash table merge overhead

performance_optimizations.technique: SIMD-accelerated hash probing for lineitem join
performance_optimizations.description: lineitem_scan_aggregation (63.8ms, 32.3%) performs hash probes into orders_ht for each lineitem row (line 240). Current: scalar probes with unordered_map::find. Optimization: Use SIMD-friendly hash table (Abseil's flat_hash_map or folly::F14FastMap) which uses SSE/AVX2 for probe operations. These tables store metadata (hash prefixes) in packed arrays, allowing SIMD-accelerated parallel probing. Also apply SIMD to the l_shipdate > date_cutoff filter (line 239) - process 8 dates at once with AVX2. Expected improvement: 15-20ms (reduce lineitem_scan_aggregation from 63.8ms to 45-50ms). Hardware supports avx512, so could potentially use 16-way SIMD.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: ~15-20ms (25-30% reduction in lineitem_scan_aggregation) - SIMD hash probing + SIMD date filtering

performance_optimizations.description: Line 37 uses madvise(addr, size, MADV_SEQUENTIAL) for all mmapped files. On HDD systems, this hint triggers aggressive readahead which can hurt random access patterns. Q3's access pattern is NOT purely sequential: (1) customer scan is sequential, (2) orders scan is sequential with zone map skips (semi-random), (3) lineitem scan is sequential. However, the orders_ht hash probes from lineitem are random access into the orders hash table (which references the mmapped orders columns). On HDD, MADV_SEQUENTIAL can cause thrashing. Recommendation: Use MADV_RANDOM for orders columns (since zone maps create skip patterns) or remove madvise hints entirely on HDD. Expected improvement: 5-10ms (reduce I/O wait in orders_filter_join).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: ~5-10ms (reduce I/O stalls) - better madvise strategy for HDD

performance_optimizations.technique: Reduce aggregation_merge overhead with parallel merge
performance_optimizations.description: aggregation_merge (10.6ms, 5.4%) serially merges 64 thread-local hash tables into final_agg (lines 266-273). Current: single-threaded iteration over all thread_maps. Optimization: Parallel tree-based merge - pair up thread maps and merge in parallel (32 pairs → 16, 16 → 8, etc. in log2(64)=6 rounds). Each merge can be done in parallel. Or use concurrent hash table for final_agg and merge in parallel. Expected improvement: 7-9ms (reduce from 10.6ms to 1-3ms with 64-way parallelism).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: ~7-9ms (70-85% reduction) - parallel merge instead of serial

performance_optimizations.description: customer_filter (22.3ms, 11.3%) builds customer_ht with ~300K entries (1.5M customers * 0.219 selectivity). Then orders_filter_join probes this hash table for 15M orders (line 194). ~78% of probes miss. Optimization: Build a Bloom filter (256KB-1MB) alongside customer_ht. Check Bloom filter first before hash table probe. This reduces cache pollution from failed probes. Expected improvement: 8-12ms (reduce orders_filter_join from 100ms to 88-92ms by avoiding negative probe overhead).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: ~8-12ms (8-12% reduction in orders_filter_join) - Bloom filter for negative probe elimination

performance_optimizations.description: lineitem_scan_aggregation (63.8ms) scans all 60M lineitem rows and filters l_shipdate > date_cutoff (line 239). Selectivity is ~65% (estimated from workload analysis). The lineitem table is sorted by l_shipdate (from storage_design.json line 27), making zone maps highly effective. Recommendation: Add zone map for l_shipdate (100K block size) and skip blocks where max_shipdate <= date_cutoff. This would skip ~35% of blocks. Expected improvement: 20-25ms (reduce lineitem_scan_aggregation from 63.8ms to 40-45ms).
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: ~20-25ms (30-40% reduction in lineitem_scan_aggregation) - zone map pruning on sorted l_shipdate

summary: Q3 iteration 1: CRITICAL CORRECTNESS BUG - zone map pruning logic is inverted (line 187), causing wrong results. Must fix before performance optimization. After fix: performance is 197.7ms vs. DuckDB target 77.8-112.8ms (2.5x slower). Bottlenecks: orders_filter_join (50.7%), lineitem_scan_aggregation (32.3%), customer_filter (11.3%). Top optimizations: (1) Fix zone map logic [CRITICAL], (2) Concurrent hash table for join to eliminate merge overhead (~10-15ms), (3) SIMD hash probing + date filtering (~15-20ms), (4) Zone map for lineitem.l_shipdate (~20-25ms), (5) Bloom filter for customer_ht (~8-12ms). Combined potential: reduce to ~90-110ms (close to DuckDB range).
